editorialSummary.text: Elegant period property featuring hand-painted ceilings, a spa & dining options, plus afternoon tea.

text.text:
This hotel is so beautiful and elegant! They have made it very upscale and gorgeous. Both the lobby bars are beautiful. The entrance is gorgeous. They have really elevated this historic building and made it a beautiful modern hotel while still staying true to the historic importance of the building.

Rooms are amazing with cozy beds and a nice bathroom. Great toiletries and I love they have cotton swabs. The beds were very comfortable. Big pillows. Plush blankets. Nice bathroom.

The pool and hot tub were lovely. Very much a vacation and a treat to stay here.

text.text:
The hotel check-in staff engaged us in a brief conversation upon arrival, which I truly appreciated and helped set a wonderful tone for our one-night stay. My sister and I were in transit from Toronto back to the United States by bus the following day, so this stop was short but memorable.

The only minor issue was that the elevator nearest our room was not connected to the lobby at the time, making access slightly inconvenient. Aside from that, everything was excellent.

It was a pleasant surprise to see the lobby decorations transition from a cherry blossom theme to a summer theme during our stay. The location was perfect—right next to Union Station, making transportation incredibly convenient.

The gym and pool facilities were outstanding. I especially enjoyed using the high-tech treadmill and following a workout guided by the virtual coach on the TV. It was a fun and unique experience.

Overall, we wish we could have stayed longer. We were also fortunate to book our room at a rate lower than the usual price, which made the experience even better. I would gladly stay here again.

text.text: Absolutely gorgeous hotel. I love the rich history and all of the timeless character that they've kept over the years. Definitely felt like a luxury hotel. Our room was so clean and all of the amenities were very nice. The slippers and bathrobe were great. We did the public parking in Royal Bank south tower and it was great that the PATH was connected right to the hotel, it was raining so the underground walk was nice. Check-in was quick and staff was helpful. Love how close it is to CN tower and Rogers Center. Definitely our favorite hotel in Toronto.

text.text:
I love this hotel. I stayed at room 4199 from the 23-26 and then extended to the 28. It was my first time, and when I first entered the rooms I did feel like the style is a bit outdated. However, once I actually interacted with everything, it is all high quality, in pristine condition, clean and functional. I also realized it fits the overall aesthetic of the hotel itself, so why change it?

I was so comfortable in that room I even extended it for 2 more days. The coffee machine works great, the bed is very comfortable. Room cleaning is thorough, and room service was beyond my expectations.

I’ve stayed in flashy new hotels that don’t give me the same vibe as this one. There are definitely a lot of business travellers but they do add a good busy vibe to the central bar, while the room areas remain quiet.

The food is amazing in this hotel, I had to force myself to eat elsewhere or I wouldn’t have explored other eats at all.

The gym also blew me away, you can have a full weightlifting workout with ample space. Beats any other hotel I’ve been at.

editorialSummary.text: Nuit & Jeff Regular's casual Northern Thai spot serving dishes like salted crab & papaya salad.

text.text:
Arrived during busy lunch hour on Wednesday afternoon. The staff were very attentive. Got a table for two within 10 minutes of waiting.
The food was very delicious!
Fresh and full of flavours.
I would highly recommend this location.
The menu items are very satisfying.
I recommend trying these dishes I ordered.
1. Spring rolls
2. Pad Thai
3. Pad Sew Ew
4. Thai fried rice
I would’ve ordered the famous Coconut ice cream however I was full.
Always a next time.
Thank you chefs.

text.text:
Delicious Thai food, made even better by the warm and attentive staff.

We waited about an hour to be seated, and since it was a busy weekend lunch, service was a bit slow at first—getting water and placing our order took some time. Once things got going, though, the experience improved.

We started with the Thai chicken appetizer, which was excellent. Our server explained the sauces, which made trying new flavors much more enjoyable.

We ordered two main dishes, and overall the food was very good. My spouse mentioned it was the best Pad Thai he’s ever had. We’d love to come back and try more dishes, preferably at a quieter time.

At first, it seemed like there wasn’t a dedicated server per table, which felt a little unusual. However, any staff member passing by checked on us, and it actually felt convenient and welcoming.

The prices were slightly on the higher side, but the service helped make it feel worth it.

text.text:
Solid place! I get why there is a consistent interest around the place, it has good food and a great ambiance. The restaurant was recommended a couple of times and I would rate the food a high 7/10.

Any test for a good Thai cuisine is spring roll starters - which were done well, crispy and not at all soggy. I ordered the khaosey and they were very well balanced. The chicken was soft and the base was tangy with good spices. The red curry was very well made too, chicken did fall dry on it but that’s more than likely because it’s cooked separately or thawed, which is very common for any restaurant.

The Thai iced tea was tasty, especially with the crushed ice, made the food enjoyable. I do feel like the food ended up being on the more expensive side, I’d say compared to suko Thai maybe 15-20$ dollars more for pretty much the same menu.

Considering the reviews, the kitchen has a good sense of spice - so any dish you do end up getting would be pretty good. Do try the kahosey

text.text: We enjoyed our dinner here.  This place is always slammed, so expect to wait, but it's a huge space so shouldn't have to wait too long.  We actually got seated right away as we said we'd sit at the bar.  For us this worked out great.  The meal was very good. We got the appetizer platter to have a little taste of everything. Saw other people ordering the appetizers separately and when it's ordered separately it looks like you get more quantity.  But it was still more than enough food for us. I just think the appetizer platter was a bit pricey. Everything else was very reasonably priced. I had 2 cocktails which were made well and tasted great.  Our two main meals were very good. Atmosphere is pretty cool, but very loud so I definitely would not call this a romantic place but great for fun conversation. Make reservations if you're in a group. Overall very good Thai food. Would return!

text.text:
been here a couple times now, i love coming during happy hour, usually get to save about $5 a dish from 2-5pm. our server maya was great, she was sweet and had good customer service.
i’ve always had a good experience but the last time i came, the food took over 45 minutes to arrive. when i first got in, there weren’t many people, but soon after the place started to fill up and somehow everyone got their food except my table. our steak came out cold, but my chicken was hot. i wish they would have not cooked the steak so quickly if it meant that the “chicken” takes long yet the people after me received their chicken before i did. we got the steak medium rare, but majority of the steak was well done in our opinion, there was no pink (more pink in the photo) and it was the driest, flavourless steak ever. i also had a super hard time cutting through the steak, so that was very upsetting.
the salmon aburi was alright, having the cheese and jalapeño on top made the aburi very biter. looking at the fish from under, the type of salmon they used didn’t look too good.
overall combining with my other visits, this place is great to come to, just disappointed from the last visit a little

text.text: I went out to dinner with friends with no big intentions, just a little outing in a nice place. Unfortunately 2 of my friends couldn’t make it and the dinner was therefore delayed by an hour. Fortunately, our waitress was very understanding, patient and kind. She asked me if we were celebrating something special to which I said no. She therefore proceeded to serve us and ask for my ID, she probably saw my date of birth in the latter. The dinner went very well the service was excellent and the food divine. I took the Bolognese Rigatonni but I can tell you that it was absolutely delicious. Unfortunately I don't remember the name of the waitress but she was absolutely adorable. At the end of our dinner she asked us to wait a little because there was a little surprise. Something I didn't expect since I didn't mention my birthday at all but it deeply touched my heart. I thank her very much for the kind and sweet attention. May her wonderful soul be blessed as she made me feel for my birthday.🙏 Ps: if you ever see this, your smile is everything, keep shining🥰

editorialSummary.text: Airy, spacious mini-chain for pub grub & craft beer plus arcade games, pool tables & live acts.

text.text:
Tried the Fish & Chips at The Rec Room and was disappointed with the taste and quality.

The Fish & Chips is listed as a popular dish on the menu, but I found it to be just average. The fish was overly crispy on the outside and was not as flavourful as I expected. The fries were cold, soggy, and lacked seasoning. The mustard-caper dill sauce served with the dish tasted a bit pungent. Overall, the dish felt overpriced at $18.

I ended the meal with the Warm Chocolate Cake, which was decent.

Service was good overall, and the server was friendly and professional.

text.text:
The Intercontinental is my go-to hotel in Toronto. The service upon check in to check out is top notch. David is one of the best people you can have to take care of you at check-in; he gives you many tips, ideas for experiences, and even shares his own experiences (you can't get better). Even more important- when you come back, he greets you with the same welcoming smile. I truly appreciate him (cheers!).

Onto the rooms: absolutely fabulous: they are clean, the beds and pillows are very comfortable, there is space to work if you have to (with hi-speed wifi), and chairs for relaxation. Also, the windows are floor to ceiling and views tend to be nice because of the location (near everything).

Additionally, the hotel has Club offerings, gym, spa, restaurant, and room service - while I haven't used these services when I have the time in future visits, I will.

I highly recommend considering (and booking) the Intercontinental.

My stay at the Shangri-La Toronto was outstanding from start to finish. From the moment I arrived, I was welcomed with warmth, professionalism, and genuine hospitality. The staff consistently provided exceptional service and made every effort to ensure my stay was comfortable and enjoyable.

The hotel is beautifully designed, with an elegant and sophisticated atmosphere that immediately makes you feel at ease. My room was immaculate, spacious, and thoughtfully appointed, with luxurious finishes and a very comfortable bed. Every detail reflected the high standards that Shangri-La is known for.

The location is also excellent, making it easy to explore downtown Toronto while enjoying a peaceful and refined retreat at the end of the day. What impressed me most was the level of care and attention provided by the team. Every interaction was friendly, professional, and personalized. They provide pillow options and weighted blankets and even specialty bath products.

This was truly a five-star experience, and I would highly recommend the Shangri-La Toronto to anyone looking for luxury, comfort, and exceptional service. I look forward to returning on my next visit to the city.

text.text: Dear Management team,  I am writing to share my recent experience at Shangri-la Toronto during the Cathy Pacific inspired high tea on March 29, which I attended to celebrate a friend's birthday.  while the ambiance was elegant and enjoyable-with live piano music and seating near the firepalce-the quality of the food was deeply disappointing and did not meet the expecatations associated with your establishment.  The steamed items were served cold upon arrival.  After bring this to the attention of staff and return the items, it was send back still bold, not even lukewarm.  This reaised concerns about the freshness and handling of the dim sum food.  I was further surprised to be informed that these items were not intended to be eaten hot.  Additionally, the scones were excessively dry and crumbly, detracting from what should have been a highlight of the high tea experience.  Under different circumstances, I would have returned the entire tower of food.  However, I chose not to disrupt my friend's birthday celebration.  Give the reputation of Shangri-La Toront as a 5 star hotel, I expected a much higher standard of food quality and preparation.  My portion of the bill exceeded $200, which I found difficult to justify in light of the experience.  I felt it was important to share this feedback.  I hope it will be taken constructively to improve and maintain the standards yur guest expect.  Thank you for your attention to this matter.

I was very disappointed with my stay, especially given that this hotel is marketed as a five-star property. The mattress in our original room was visibly deteriorating and had multiple stains. This is not what I expect at this level.

I do want to acknowledge that the staff was kind and professional. Once we reported the issue, they promptly moved us to a different room, which I appreciated. However, it was still difficult to feel comfortable knowing that some rooms are clearly not being maintained to a five-star standard.

Unfortunately, I will not be returning. I typically stay at another five-star hotel in Toronto and decided to try something new this time. That choice didn’t work out, and I left feeling very dissatisfied with the overall condition of the hotel.

text.text:
This was our first time trying Haidilao Hot Pot with a toddler, and it was such a fun and memorable experience.

The food was fresh, with a great variety of meats,  and sides, and we loved being able to customize our own sauces at the sauce station. The option to choose multiple soup broths in one pot made it perfect for everyone at the table.

What really stood out was the service. The staff were incredibly attentive, and the live noodle-stretching performance added such a fun touch—our toddler absolutely loved it.
The restaurant is also very family-friendly, with high chairs that fit right at the table and thoughtful little details for kids.

Even the bathrooms were spotless and well-equipped with extra toiletries, mouth wash, and kids sink.

The best part, you can choose up to four different broth in a metal devider.

You can really tell they care about the overall customer experience. Highly recommend, especially for families or anyone looking for a fun dining experience!

text.text:
Have been here probably more than a dozen time over the past 4 years for both a la carte and ayce. Don’t get me wrong, the food is amazing, but for the price? the a la carte is really not worth all that. The buffets, on the other hand, are a different story since I am willing to starve myself all day for them.

The best deals from HDL have been the $2.95 Marbled Beef and the Meat Carnival, but sadly both of these absolute steals are no longer available at this location. The Flexi-Semi Buffet is great too, but it would be so much better if it included the Flat Iron Steak in addition to the current protein options. I personally think the soup bases are way way overpriced so I recommend just getting water to save money since the sauce bar (for $3.99) provides all the flavoring you’d need tbh + unlimited fruits.

But hey, I’m a uni student so if you’re balling unlike me, go ahead and splurge. Maybe consider giving me a call if you are feeling generous too and want to feed a broke big back. I promise to eat your money’s worth.

Overall, a great experience with wonderful staff—we will definitely be back!

text.text: My son and I were in town for a convention and decided to give this place a try. I have always wanted to try it but am never in the city. We were glad we did. The restaurant itself was very uniquely decorated with little booths on a train car, booths made out of old beds and elevators and it had a very old fashioned vibe. We went pretty late and it was still decently busy. It was a little loud making it difficult to hear our server and he seemed a little rude and irritated but that was the only thing we had a small issue with. The food was fantastic and decently priced for what we got. We got one of those all inclusive meals, it cost $25 and we got fresh bread with whipped garlic butter, a starter salad (or you can get soup), a big bowl of spaghetti with meat sauce (there are other options), a good heaping of ice cream (3 flavours to choose from) and a tea (or coffee). It was all delicious and a generous portion size, we were so stuffed by the end. One downside is parking, however, that is pretty much how it is everywhere you go in the city. They did have a paid underground parking lot pretty much directly across the street so that worked other than having to pay for parking on top of your meal. Overall, I would definitely recommend and can't wait to go back.

text.text:
Our server Mackenzie was amazing!
We had a big party on my wedding day last Saturday, and everything was perfect.
We tried to reserve a brunch table for around 12–15 people and called many restaurants, but we couldn’t find one that matched what we were looking for. Most places charge extra service fees, require minimum spending, or don’t accept groups over 10 people — but not here!
This is honestly the best restaurant I’ve found after living here for 4 years. The prices are great, the food is delicious, and the service was perfect. The atmosphere is classic and beautiful.
Even though we didn’t order as much as we normally would (because we hadn’t seen our friends in a long time and spent most of the time talking), we stayed for about two hours and Mackenzie kept checking on us to make sure we had everything we needed. I really appreciated her kindness and attention.
I hope to come back for dinner soon.
Thank you so much, Mackenzie — I hope to see you again! :)

Service was good, and the restaurant itself is beautifully decorated. The lighting creates a pleasant atmosphere, but the noise level was a little high, making it difficult for conversation.

Overall, I had a very enjoyable experience and would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for quality Italian food in Toronto.

Absolutely loved Scaddabush! This is definitely one of those restaurants I know I’ll keep coming back to.

The atmosphere was beautiful, warm, and lively, perfect for a relaxed lunch or dinner with friends. The service was smooth, the food came out fresh, and everything felt high quality from start to finish.

The pasta was honestly one of the best pastas I’ve had in a long time. Rich flavor, perfectly cooked, creamy without being too heavy, and just incredibly satisfying. I’ll definitely be coming back just to try more of their pasta dishes.

Even my friend, who usually never orders pasta and isn’t really a pasta person at all, tried it and was genuinely impressed. She kept tasting mine and said next time she’s definitely ordering pasta for herself!

The garlic bread itself was soft rather than crispy, but honestly the garlic flavor and the sauce were delicious that it made it special.

Overall, such a great experience. Beautiful space, delicious food, and definitely a restaurant worth returning to again and again.

text.text:
I recently visited Koh Lipe Thai Kitchen, a Michelin Guide listed restaurant in downtown Toronto.

The restaurant focuses on Southern Thai cuisine, which is quite different from the Northern Thai style that many Toronto restaurants serve.

We ordered several dishes. The Hor Mok Roulade (steamed fish curry mousse) was delicate and fragrant with coconut milk and lime.

The Mee Puu (blue crab curry with vermicelli) was visually striking because the noodles are naturally dyed with butterfly pea flower, and the curry itself was rich and flavorful.

The Choo Chee Pla Too (mackerel in red curry) had a creamy coconut curry sauce that paired well with rice.

For dessert, the Pandan Toast with pandan custard and coconut ice cream was very enjoyable, and the Mango Sticky Rice was classic and well balanced.

Overall, the flavors were bold, spicy, and aromatic—very representative of Southern Thai cuisine. Service was good, but the restaurant gets quite busy, so reservations are recommended.

Expect around $50 per person depending on what you order.

I have been many times before. Unfortunately this time was disappointing. As soon as we sat we were told of a 1.5 hour table limit. So we were time conscious. The food came out quickly, again to encourage us the not linger.
The restaurant felt very grungy and the food wasn’t flavourful. Lack of chilly on the mango salad (which is a must), the stinky pea curry tasted very powdery and not left to marinate properly. It was prepared in a rush.
The mango  sticky rice-though presents well -it should be written it’s presented in a white chocolate shell. I don’t like white chocolate so it was an unpleasant surprise.
Due to the time limit I felt we were eating take out. We were in and out in 40 mins. Thoughts to the owners: Slow things down folks, we spent over $150 for 3 people, let us enjoy the visit.

text.text:
Michelin guide restaurant located on the popular Baldwin St area behind hospital row.

The restaurant has a nice warm vibe. The walls have beautiful artwork and there are hanging decorations.  There's a heated and covered patio to allow additional diners.  The second floor has additional seating and washrooms.

The food was excellent. My wife got the chicken pad thai and I got the Pad Gra Pao. The pad thai has good portion of chicken, tofu, noodles. The Pad Gra pao has fried pieces of pork belly and fried egg on top of the rice. One word, delicious.  Only gripe I had was the steamed rice was cold.

The server is warm and welcoming and we enjoyed our experience here. I would recommend a visit here if you are looking for some authentic Thai comfort food.

text.text: I was at King Taps today for an impromptu post city hall wedding luncheon. We were a group of 7 without a reservation anywhere (crazy, right?!). We called King Taps and they magically had a table available right at the beginning of happy hour. As soon as we walked in, the entire staff recognized the occasion. Not only did every staff walk by with a congratulatory message for the couple, the team went ABOVE AND BEYOND to make our group feel special. Our seating timeframe was waved, we received comp’d celebratory bubbly and comp’d dessert with a congratulatory message written in powdered sugar. On top of that, the couple received a congratulatory card that all of the restaurant staff had signed! Did I mention we DID NOT have a reservation?! I was totally blown away by the level of care and hospitality shown by the entire King Taps team. On top of that, service was excellent and the food was great! I’ve been to King Taps many times for less of an occasion and have always been happy - this time I was truly blown away. On behalf of our entire family, we want to thank the servers, chefs, managers and all supporting staff for making the day feel special. My little sister was absolutely in awe, her friends were over the moon & my parents had a fantastic lunch with 0% stress. Everyone in group is excited to share with our friends and family how awesome our day was. Thanks again team! Excited to come back again!

text.text:
Decent meals in a very energetic and lively atmosphere. We unknowingly visited on a night with a live DJ, and while I can appreciate the craft, and without being too much of a prude, it was perhaps a bit too loud for the relatively tight interior space which made it incredibly difficult to hold a conversation.

The nachos appetizer came out about 3 minutes prior to the mains and the table was not big enough for everything. Once we got everything Tetris'd on, it was unfortunately not extraordinary (albeit decent). The nachos had great toppings which were evenly distributed, my big mac pizza had a unique flavour, one could say almost akin to an actual big mac, but it came out narrowly scalding hot. My partner's chicken sandwich was met with a "just okay" reaction and looked fairly mediocre.

I will say our waitress was very friendly and very helpful with getting everything we needed, and the prices seemed reasonable.

text.text:
Great $15 pasta special on Wednesdays! Seriously, don’t know where else you can get such a great deal in the downtown core.

Came for pre-show dinner, as it’s in a prime location within 5 min walk of all the mirvish theaters and Roy Thompson hall.

Since it was still before 7pm, we got to enjoy a bunch of apps that were discounted, bang bang shrimp, truffle fries, and of course need some greens, so got the charred broccoli. All were good, would order again. I can definitely see myself coming back for just drinks and apps post work.

Most of the pasta specials were cream based, but that’s usually my go-to when eating out. Between the three of us, we shared the lobster pasta and the smoky bacon spaghetti. Tbh I loved the lobster sauce but would have preferred it on spaghetti vs the pappardelle (long flat). The smoky bacon was also good, but how can one pass up a lobster option ;).

Not much of a drinker, but the mocktail selection was very good (and reasonably priced). Ordered the temple run which had nice tropical flavour which went well with the apps we ordered.

Highly recommend a reservation since the place was packed! On that note, pretty tight quarters, and you have to go up a few steps to get to the bathroom, so not very wheelchair friendly.

All in all great experience, we were in and out in just over an hour which was perfect timing to make our 7:30pm show at the nearby princess of Wales theater.

text.text:
Stopped by Matcha Matcha and tried their matcha latte and hojicha latte—both were honestly so well done. The matcha was smooth, vibrant, and not overly bitter, while the hojicha had this really nice roasted, almost nutty flavour that stood out. I appreciate that neither drink was overly sweet—you can actually taste the quality of the tea.
The overall aesthetic and vibe of the place is on point as well. It’s definitely on the pricier side, but if you’re into premium matcha, it’s worth it for the experience.

text.text: I was so excited to order the Dubai Chocolate cookie that was advertised for the week of January 25-31. I placed my order for a box of 6 mini and 1 regular size on January 27 well in advance of the delivery on January 30. I ordered 3 of the Dubai chocolate cookies (2 small + 1 regular) as I had plan to surprise my daughters with the cookies. When I got the cookies on January 30, I got 3 of Chocolate Hazelnut Cheesecake ft. NUTELLA instead and no Dubai chocolate cookies. I phoned the store right away and the staff was telling so casually that they ran out of the ingredients for the Dubai chocolate like 2 days ago. Then, I questioned him why would you not contact your customer to inform them of that before making a replacement without my knowledge as I could have made a decision for the replacement. The staff did not seem to care that the customer did not get what they paid for. I told him how disappointed I was and that I should have been informed in advance, then I could decide what to do. He seemed very impatient with my call and did not sound apologetic at all but in the end, he finally offered me a refund for the 3 Dubai Chocolate cookies only. Somehow, the store ran out of boxes as I got the 6 cookies in 2 separate boxes instead. I will not buy from this store again and this is not the way a business should treat their customers. I am not sure if I will get the refund yet and will have to wait and see if the staff has actually issued a refund.

text.text:
Our first visit to Black+Blue was, overall, a beautiful dining experience. The atmosphere, service pacing, and quality of the food were all excellent. However, a few moments during the evening left me genuinely confused and unfortunately changed my impression by the end of the night.

Before arriving, we had already looked through photos online and saved a few dishes we were excited to order. We showed our server screenshots of a particular steak with sides, a Caesar salad, and a lemon tart dessert.

The Caesar salad was available, but we were told the steak we wanted was no longer offered and that the closest alternative would be the Cowboy Ribeye, which happened to be one of the more premium-priced steaks on the menu. Later, when we asked about the lemon tart, we were again told it was unavailable, so we selected another dessert instead.

What made the experience disappointing was that, on our way out, we noticed another table being served the exact lemon tart we had asked for earlier. We also saw another table with the same steak and sides combination we had originally requested.

At that point, it honestly left us uncertain whether there had been a misunderstanding, a communication issue, or perhaps an attempt to guide us toward higher-priced items. I truly hope it was simply a case of miscommunication, but unfortunately it changed the feeling we left with.

It’s unfortunate, because the restaurant itself is beautiful and the food was genuinely enjoyable. I just wish the ending of the evening had felt a little more honest and seamless.

text.text:
We went to Gyubee Japanese Grill for my birthday, and I have to say we all really enjoyed it. The food was great( we did the lunch menu) and my friends loved it too.

The service was also very good. The only reason I’m giving 4 stars is because we were a large group split into two tables, and they only took orders from one side. The other side had to rely on them to ask for items, which wasn’t very nice.

Aside from that, everything else was amazing. We’re already looking forward to going back to have fun and try more items.

The meat cuts are delicious. Some are quite thin, but it makes sense since they cook faster on the grill. They also gave us a 90 minute limit and recommend items based on how long they take to cook, which is helpful.

Overall, a great experience, we’ll definitely be back!
